name: American Airlines, Inc. v. Wolens
summary: Held that while the ADA preempts state consumer-protection claims against airlines, it does not bar breach-of-contract suits that merely enforce the airline's own self-imposed obligations.
significance: Carved out a limited exception allowing suits based on a party's own contractual undertakings even under broad airline deregulation preemption, which petitioner argues supports permitting tort claims not targeting regulatory standards.
